ranking.explanation: VULN-001 (Command Injection, CVSS 9.8) ranks #1 over VULN-002 (SQL Injection, identical CVSS 9.8) purely on runtime evidence: VULN-001 is under a massive, accelerating, globally-distributed attack (2349 hits/h from 23 ASNs across 9 countries, trend 6.24x vs prior rate, 100% success rate), whereas VULN-002's attack traffic originates from a single ASN in one country and is decelerating. VULN-004 (Stored XSS) outranks VULN-003 (File Upload) despite lower CVSS because it is KEV-listed with higher external pressure, while VULN-003 has zero active attack traffic and no KEV listing.

rankings.reasoning: 2349 attacks in the last 60 min from 2349 unique IPs across 23 ASNs and 9 countries — the broadest geographic and infrastructure diversity in this set. Trend ratio is 6.24x (1586 hits in the last 15 min vs a prior-period rate of 254/15-min), signalling a sharply accelerating campaign. Every recorded attempt succeeded (successful_attack_rate 1.0), confirming the endpoint is fully exploitable with no compensating controls. KEV-listed; 23 public PoCs on ExploitDB; 187 GitHub mentions in 30 days; underground chatter 0.62. Identical CVSS 9.8 to VULN-002 but ~2.3× higher composite score driven entirely by live attack evidence.

296 attacks in the last 60 min, all from a single ASN and single country (RU), consistent with a targeted sqlmap scan rather than a distributed campaign. Trend ratio 0.88x indicates the attack rate is slightly decelerating. All attempts succeeded (successful_attack_rate 1.0), confirming live exploitability. External pressure is the highest in this set: KEV-listed, 47 ExploitDB PoCs, 312 GitHub mentions in 30 days, underground chatter 0.81 — indicating the attacker community is heavily weaponizing this CWE class. The single-ASN concentration prevents this from topping the ranking, but 100% success rate and extreme external pressure make it the clear #2.

Only 1 attack hit in the last 60 min from 1 IP/1 ASN; trend ratio is 0.0x (zero activity in the last 15 min). Active exploitation score is very low (13.41). However, it is KEV-listed, carries 32 ExploitDB PoCs, 245 GitHub mentions in 30 days, and an underground chatter score of 0.45, producing the second-highest external pressure in the set (76.84). Outranks VULN-003 because VULN-003 is not KEV-listed (costs 40 external-pressure points) and has lower chatter; the single successful hit here also confirms the endpoint is reachable and exploitable. CVSS 6.1 (lowest in the set) confirms static severity is the tiebreaker, not the driver.

Zero attacks in the last 60 min across all APM dimensions — active exploitation score is 0.00. Not KEV-listed, which alone costs 40 points of external pressure; only 18 ExploitDB PoCs, 134 GitHub mentions in 30 days, and the lowest underground chatter (0.38) in the set, yielding an external pressure of 31.63. Despite a CVSS of 8.8 (highest of the non-9.8 vulns), static severity accounts for only 15% of the composite and cannot overcome zero active evidence and weak external pressure. Ranks last. Still requires patching — webshell upload is high-consequence — but there is no current attack pressure justifying emergency prioritization over the other three.

remediated.name: Command Injection in ping form
remediated.action: Generic patch: update VULN-001 package to latest safe version

remediated.name: SQL Injection in user lookup
remediated.action: Generic patch: update VULN-002 package to latest safe version

remediated.name: Stored XSS in guestbook
remediated.action: Generic patch: update VULN-004 package to latest safe version

Despite a CVSS of 8.8 (highest of the non-9.8 vulns), static severity accounts for only 15% of the composite and cannot overcome zero active evidence and weak external pressure. Ranks last. Still requires patching — webshell upload is high-consequence — but there is no current attack pressure justifying emergency prioritization over the other three.

summary: Ranking Agent triaged 4 vuln(s). 3 remediated, 1 PR(s) created, 1 deferred.
